title: Case/Care Management * Chronic/Severe Mental Illness - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Case/Care Management * Chronic/Severe Mental Illness 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Chronic/Severe Mental Illness Deafness Disabilities Issues Hard of Hearing Older Adults Hispanic/Latino Community Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Homeless Veterans Veterans Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HEALTH SERVICE BUILDING ADULT BEHAVIORAL HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment, individual, family, and group therapy, case management, skills training, peer specialist support, psychiatric services, and medication management. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with a severe and persistent mental illness Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 766-6767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHELTERCARE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers specialized, wraparound behavioral health services. Services are customized for each individual and often include counseling, skills training, case management, peer support, and support with community navigation. 499 W 4th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Adults with OHP or Medicaid coverage, a diagnosis of a serious mental illness, and difficulty managing activities of daily living Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 505-5188 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ADULT BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SHELTERCARE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SERVICES
